vocabulary.example: It was on the corner of the street that he noticed the first sign of something peculiar -- a cat reading a map.
vocabulary.grammarTip: Often used when something seems odd but you cannot explain it right away.

vocabulary.example: involved in anything strange or mysterious, because they just didn't hold with such nonsense.

vocabulary.definition: to shake because you feel fear or disgust
vocabulary.example: The Dursleys shuddered to think what the neighbors would say if the Potters arrived in the street.

vocabulary.definition: a loose outer garment worn over clothes
a person with no magical powers
vocabulary.example: He also thought he had been called a Muggle, whatever that was.

vocabulary.definition: the son of your brother or sister
vocabulary.example: Come to think of it, he wasn't even sure his nephew was called Harry.

vocabulary.definition: to feel or show great joy
vocabulary.example: Rejoice, for You-Know-Who has gone at last!

vocabulary.definition: to stay alive after something dangerous
vocabulary.example: how in the name of heaven did Harry survive?

vocabulary.definition: a mark left on the skin after a wound heals
a cat with striped or spotted fur
vocabulary.example: There was a tabby cat standing on the corner of Privet Drive,

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: A baby boy who survives an attack and becomes famous in the wizarding world.

characters.name: The Dursleys
characters.description: Harry's aunt, uncle, and cousin who want to seem perfectly normal.

characters.name: Professor McGonagall
characters.description: A witch who watches Privet Drive as a tabby cat before meeting Dumbledore.

characters.name: Dumbledore
characters.description: The head wizard who leaves baby Harry on the Dursleys' doorstep.

characters.name: Hagrid
characters.description: A giant who brings Harry to Privet Drive on a flying motorcycle.

background.hook: On an ordinary Tuesday, the Dursleys try to ignore strange signs — whispering people in cloaks, a cat reading a map, and news about You-Know-Who. By nightfall, Dumbledore and McGonagall leave baby Harry on their doorstep.
background.readingFocus: Notice how the Dursleys react to anything strange. Watch for the first clues about Harry's past and why wizards celebrate.

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: How baby Harry came to live with the Dursleys and why he is famous
A Quidditch match in the wizarding world
The Dursleys' vacation to the beach

comprehension.explanation: The chapter moves from the Dursleys' ordinary day to the attack on Harry's parents, his survival, and Dumbledore leaving him on Privet Drive.
The chapter opens with Mr. and Mrs. Dursley of number four, Privet Drive.

comprehension.stem: Why do the Dursleys dislike talking about the Potters?

options.text: They are ashamed of their magical relatives

comprehension.explanation: Mrs. Dursley pretended she did not have a sister because the Potters were as unDursleyish as possible.

comprehension.stem: What mark does baby Harry have on his forehead?

options.text: A cut shaped like a bolt of lightning

comprehension.explanation: McGonagall sees a curiously shaped cut, like a bolt of lightning, on Harry's forehead.

comprehension.stem: How do wizards in the street feel when they hear You-Know-Who is gone?
comprehension.explanation: An old man in a violet cloak shouts, "Rejoice, for You-Know-Who has gone at last!"

comprehension.stem: Why does Dumbledore leave Harry with the Dursleys?

options.text: Harry has family there and will be safer growing up away from fame
The Dursleys asked to adopt him
Harry chose to live with them

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore says Harry is better off growing up away from his fame until he is ready for Hogwarts.

comprehension.stem: How does Hagrid bring baby Harry to Privet Drive?
comprehension.explanation: A huge motorcycle falls out of the air and lands on the road; Hagrid is riding it with baby Harry.

vocabulary.explanation: Mr. Dursley notices something peculiar — a cat reading a map.

vocabulary.stem: On the corner of Privet Drive, Mr. Dursley saw a ___ cat, but there wasn't a map in sight.
vocabulary.explanation: The tabby cat on the corner is later revealed to be Professor McGonagall.

options.text: teaches Defence Against the Dark Arts

vocabulary.explanation: Mr. Dursley is a Muggle who knows nothing about magic.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'mysterious'?
vocabulary.explanation: The Dursleys do not hold with anything strange or mysterious.

vocabulary.stem: The man in the violet cloak cried, "___, for You-Know-Who has gone at last!"
vocabulary.explanation: Rejoice means to celebrate — wizards are relieved that You-Know-Who is gone.

cloze.title: The Boy Who Lived
paragraphs: Mr. and Mrs. Dursley were proud to say they were perfectly (1) ___, thank you very much. They were the last people you'd expect to be involved in anything strange or (2) ___.
On his way to work, Mr. Dursley noticed something (3) ___ — a cat reading a map. Later he saw people in (4) ___ whispering excitedly together.
Professor McGonagall asked how Harry could possibly (5) ___. Dumbledore said the boy would be (6) ___ — a legend — and that he would keep his (7) ___ forever.

gaps.explanation: The Dursleys want to seem perfectly normal.
They dislike anything strange or mysterious.
Mr. Dursley sees the first sign of something peculiar on the corner.
Mr. Dursley is disturbed to see people in cloaks in broad daylight.
McGonagall asks how Harry survived the attack.
Dumbledore says Harry will be famous — a legend.
Dumbledore says Harry will have that scar forever.
